changes.reason: Activate exactly one dormant VALID_0DTE pin-avoid attribution profile as a full router replacement. This tests whether existing charm/pin tail states can be split by router attribution instead of broad global veto tightening, while leaving no-0DTE and 1DTE routing untouched.

Enable the dormant charm-wall pin-avoid concept for a single-profile discovery run. Current charm_pin_pullback_long owns the -513.4 worst MAE while charm_wall_breakaway_long is high-convexity but small-sample; this probes whether a pin-avoid branch can absorb or reclassify that tail without touching parent profile thresholds.

recommendation.expected_effect: Expected to improve attribution around VALID_0DTE charm/pin tails and potentially reduce combined charm-pin/charm-wall heat without deleting the proven pullback, squeeze, 1DTE, or no-0DTE edges. Tradeoff is router cannibalization: reject if total net profit drops below 98% of the leader/base, protected fast extended-VWAP winners are displaced, or the activated profile adds chop without standalone positive expectancy.
recommendation.label: promote
recommendation.rationale: Use the dormant pin-avoid router probe as one logical config-only family. The latest run has strong full-sample quality but the worst MAE remains concentrated in charm_pin_pullback_long, and broad static filters would cut protected winners. This experiment follows the strategist plan: activate one dormant concept only, make no global veto changes, preserve current option-data routing, and measure whether an existing C# branch can separate pin/tail behavior cleanly.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: charm_pin_pullback_long contributes $1200.3 across 32 VALID_0DTE trades with median hold near 5 minutes, but owns the global -513.4 MAE. Existing broad filters overlap protected extended-VWAP winners, while dormant pin-avoid routing may provide attribution without pruning the whole parent edge.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Charm Wall Pin-Avoid Attribution Split
strategy_hypotheses.risk: The dormant profile may be unreachable, may cannibalize profitable charm_pin_pullback_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long trades, or may add low-quality pin chop if its internal trigger is too loose.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: VALID_0DTE charm-wall or charm-pin context where current charm_pin_pullback_long/charm_wall_breakaway_long entries face pin-avoid or tail-risk behavior.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Activated profile has positive standalone PnL or improves combined charm-pin/charm-wall worst MAE, total net profit remains >=98% of leader/base, protected fast-winner retention >=95%, and slow_12h remains 0.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: Worst tails show path dependence: delayed first profit and MFE-after-MAE behavior in charm_pin_pullback_long and pullback_long. Static config thresholds either retain too little PnL or fail to improve worst MAE cleanly.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Early-Progress Heat Classifier
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Can overfit a handful of delayed recoveries and block clean fast scalps unless validated against protected winner UTCs and out-of-sample days.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long or pullback_long entries that fail to make meaningful positive progress before MAE in the first few minutes.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Worst MAE improves toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% combined charm-pin/pullback PnL, >=95% protected fast winners, total net profit >=98%, and slow_12h remains 0.
